danger_signals.meaning: Combat encounter required; can drop bones as currency when defeated

danger_signals.visual_form: Heart container loss (player starts with three hearts)
danger_signals.meaning: Damage taken from enemies or hazards; losing all hearts = life lost and level restart

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger distinct enemy sprites at level endpoints
danger_signals.meaning: Major danger milestone; required defeat to progress
Damage or death risk from navigation; varied by level design

safety_signals.meaning: Safe respawn point established; player does not restart from level beginning
Temporary invincibility granted; protection from enemy damage
Permanent health increase; raises maximum hearts available
Safe commerce zone; ability to spend bones currency for items, level skips, or save game

safety_signals.visual_form: Spear and axe collectibles (replace default spiked club)
safety_signals.meaning: Combat capability enhancement; limited ammo but improved damage

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Village Chief summons player; NPCs provide hints and tutorial; shops indicate settlement presence

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple dinosaur and prehistoric enemy sprite types throughout levels
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scattered throughout 23 levels; active antagonists who stole village food supply

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player tasked with collecting food items throughout levels; food tallied at level end with percentage scoring
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Chief summons Sam to gather food; survival mission narrative frames all gameplay

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dinosaur enemies populate each level as persistent obstacles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies must be defeated for bones (currency); their theft of food establishes antagonistic presence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bones serve as in-game currency; limited weapon ammo; finite shields and hearts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must manage resources strategically; bone collection from defeated enemies drives economic system

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 23 side-scrolling levels with escalating difficulty; checkpoint system shows safe progress points
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Checkpoint activation mid-level; letter collection (B-O-N-U-S) unlocks bonus areas; boss defeats mark major milestones

environmental_storytelling.example: Village Chief summons and tasks Sam; NPCs provide hints and tutorial context for player actions and world situation
Food items scattered throughout levels communicate survival theme; bones dropped by enemies establish enemy-resource connection
23 sequential levels advance Sam's quest to gather food before winter; checkpoint system shows established safe zones within wild territory
Multiple dinosaur types scattered through levels visually establish Stone Age setting and ongoing predatory threat
Shops with merchants provide mechanical explanation for resource spending and gameplay flexibility (level skips, saves)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Runs 'like a dog' for faster movement
Wielding spiked club (default) or alternative weapons (spear, axe with limited ammo)
Shout ability defeats all enemies currently on screen
Temporary invincibility after stone age shield collection
Patrol and attack; drop bones on defeat
Summons Sam; establishes survival narrative at game start
Accept bones currency; offer level skips, game saves, and equipment

notes: Prehistorik Man uses a straightforward prehistoric survival narrative to structure its platformer mechanics. Visual communication relies heavily on: (1) enemy sprite identification and distribution to establish threat presence; (2) collectible items (food, bones, hearts, shields, weapons) as visual representations of resources and power progression; (3) checkpoint markers as visual safe-zone indicators; (4) NPC presence to anchor narrative context. The game lacks faction complexity—binary opposition between player-aligned village and antagonistic dinosaurs. World-state communication is primarily mechanical (food collection, bone economy) rather than visual ambiance shifts. Level variety ('gimmicks' including vehicles and key-item puzzles) provides implicit environmental storytelling through gameplay changes. Regional variants and anti-piracy measures documented but not directly relevant to manifestation layer analysis.
